metadata.title:
HELATEST
EXHIBITION


Of NERVE"

Cant Get Transfers Until
Transfer Point Is


Reached.

pages.raw_text:
Whether it is jim Hill in
Fuence or whatever else it
may be, the new rule Of the
Spokane Traction Co., refus-
ing to give transfers except
at certain points along it's
lines, has raised cain with
patrons of that company.


Two or three fights have
already occurred, one con-
ductor has been choked until
his tongue stuck out, and in
spectors ride the lines trav-
e!ed by the "insurgents," "
ready to eject them from the
ears in case any more resist
ance to the new order is at
tempted.


An appeal was made to Mayor





powerless. that is was question of
law. in spite of the fact that the
franchise of every street railway
line in the city eXacts that each
passenger who pays a 5-cent fare
shall be entitled to a transfer.


The trouble started among some
of the employees of the planning
mills on the north side, who live
on the south side. The new rule Of
the company requires that transfers
be issued only at the transfer
points, and not when the fare is
paid as was formerly done, and is
done daily by the Washington
Water Power company. Under this
rule men who mounted the cars on
the north side and were compelled
to transfer at Second and Wash
ington never found a seat on the
outbound cars. for the reason that
everything was taken when the
cars reached there. Since the Hill
people took control of the Traction
system the car service has grown
worse, and the order of strap hang
ers has increased to great ex-
tent. His is running the system for
the money there is in it, in an eff
fort to increase the dividends. The
men from the north side shops
wanted to transfer at Main and
Washington, in order that they
might be enabled to get a seat or
for the further. reason that they
might be enab1ed to get something
for the home without paying an
other fare.


There are five in the band


of "insurgents" fighting this in


lqultous rule. One of the most


aggressive Of the bunch the


first night Of resistance de-


manded transfer when he paid


his fare. The car was held up
